extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primož won from early breakaway, soloing over Telegraph/Galibier and descending to finish, while FROOME gained time on GC rivals on final Galibier accelerations.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC attacked repeatedly on climbs within breakaway before Telegraph/Galibier, isolating himself early rather than waiting for final climb.
MARTIN Dan's repeated accelerations on Galibier for Sky intentionally softened rivals before FROOME's decisive move, team-coordinated attrition pattern.
BARGUIL Warren attacked mid-Galibier for KOM points while still in GC group, prioritizing jersey over position.
SKY placed LANDA with FROOME in finale group, maintaining 2-rider numerical advantage that deterred coordinated counter-attacks.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: positive: crashed in breakaway but remounted and still had power to solo from Telegraph, exceptional stage-racing durability signal
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: responded to BARDET/URAN attacks directly without teammate aid, high-altitude acceleration strength despite being GC leader
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: definitively dropped on Galibier accelerations and never returned to lead group, altitude weakness exposed
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: repeatedly distanced by each acceleration, losing 9 seconds to FROOME/URAN/BARDET despite shorter gap at stage start
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: multiple attacks on Galibier at altitude forced FROOME to respond personally, offensive threat despite time deficit
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: gaining green jersey via KDO crash reveals late-tour strength progression, climbing into points range
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: attacked for Sky as domestique on Galibier, revealing team depth and willingness to sacrifice GC position
